text: Abstract High-dimensional credit card fraud data is a common case in fraud detection using machine learning. Feature selection methods are widely used to improve the performance of the models. In this work, we investigate the performance of classification models using the features selected based on SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values and the model’s feature importance list. We trained five different types of classifiers: XGBoost, Decision Tree, CatBoost, Extremely Randomized Trees and Random Forest on the features selected based on the two methods and observed the performance of the models using the Area under the Precision-Recall Curve (AUPRC) metric. The experiments were conducted on the Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Detection Dataset. The results and statistical tests show that the feature selection methods based on the importance values outperform the SHAP values for all classifiers and for all feature subset sizes. Using model’s feature importance list in the features are selected for large datasets and hence it is recommended to use the model’s feature importance list than the SHAP values as calculating the SHAP feature importance is a separate task and the model’s feature importance list is available without any extra effort. Hence, for larger models and datasets, the model’s feature importance list will be more efficient.
headline: Fully Human Written
